summary: Ukrainian drone operations have knocked out power in Sevastopol, while Russian forces reported strikes across Sumy, Kharkiv, and the Pavlovgrad district. Both sides continue extensive UAV activity, with multiple sightings in Sumy, Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, and Zaporizhzhia regions. Ground combat persists in several eastern villages with small‑arms engagements.
key_points: Ukrainian drones caused a complete power outage in Sevastopol after multiple strikes on the main substation, igniting a fire visible for kilometers (Source: @UkraineNow/82140).
Russian forces conducted overnight strikes on Sumy, Kharkiv, and Pavlovgrad district, according to a two‑major report (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
Ukrainian assault units are engaged in small‑arms battles in Shostka district (Bachevsk) and villages Ivolzhansk, Pysarevka, and Novaya Sech in Sumy region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
Ukrainian drones attacked civilian targets in Belgorod region, wounding civilians in Aleksandrovka, Grayvoron, and Proletarsky (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed near Aviatorske in Dnipropetrovsk and over Sumy region near Hotin and Boromlya (Source: @eRadarrua/98053; @kpszsu/66233).
Russian tactical aviation activity was reported on the north‑east direction, indicating potential air strikes near front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/66265).
Ukrainian forces reported using the domestically produced "Vyravnyvatel" guided aerial bomb, capable of 10‑140 km range (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31125).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian drone strikes have left Sevastopol without power, highlighting intensified aerial operations. Russian forces claim successful overnight strikes on Sumy, Kharkiv, and Pavlovgrad, while ground fighting continues in multiple eastern villages. Both sides report heightened UAV activity across several oblasts.

points: Ukrainian operators from the 291st Motorized Rifle Regiment are active in the Zaporozhye area, targeting Ukrainian snipers in cellars (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31119).
Ukrainian forces have begun employing the "Vyravnyvatel" 250 kg guided aerial bomb, with claimed production and launch ranges up to 140 km (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31125).
Ukrainian drones caused a total power outage in Sevastopol after six‑seven strikes on the main substation, resulting in a fire visible for many kilometres (Source: @UkraineNow/82140).
Ukrainian assault units continue small‑arms battles in Shostka district (Bachevsk) and villages Ivolzhansk, Pysarevka, and Novaya Sech in Sumy region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
Ukrainian UAV sightings include a reconnaissance UAV near Aviatorske in Dnipropetrovsk and multiple UAVs north‑west of Hotin heading south‑west (Source: @eRadarrua/98053; @kpszsu/66233).
Russian forces reported overnight strikes on Sumy, Kharkiv, and Pavlovgrad district, according to a two‑major briefing (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
Russian drones attacked civilian targets in Belgorod region, wounding civilians in Aleksandrovka, Grayvoron, and Proletarsky (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
Russian tactical aviation activity was noted on the north‑east direction, indicating possible air‑strike operations near front‑line zones (Source: @kpszsu/66265).
Russian Ministry of Defense reported successful assaults in Krasny Lyman and Konstantinovka, clearing 52 and 128 buildings per day respectively (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
Russian forces claim a man injured by an enemy drone in 1st Yankovo, Rylsk district, Kursk region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
Ukrainian small‑arms fighting continues in Shostka district (Bachevsk) and villages Ivolzhansk, Pysarevka, Novaya Sech in Sumy region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
Ukrainian assault units are active in Kazachya Lopan, Losevka, Ukrainskoe, Petro‑Ivanovka, and surrounding areas on the Kharkiv direction (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
Russian forces report progress in Zaoskolye from the Kupyansk direction and ongoing battles in Kupyansk‑Vuzlovy (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
Ukrainian drone strikes target Stepnogorsk and Prymorsk on the Zaporozhye front, with continuous UAV activity reported (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
In the Kherson region, five civilians, including a child, were wounded over the previous 24 hours amid ongoing attacks (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
UAVs observed north of Zaporizhzhia from the south‑west, and another heading toward Zaporizhzhia from the south‑east (Source: @kpszsu/66258; @kpszsu/66274).
UAV activity reported on the northern outskirts of Sumy, heading west or south‑west (Source: @kpszsu/66271).
UAVs detected near the north‑west of Hotin, moving south‑west toward Mikolaivka/Ulyanivka (Source: @kpszsu/66233).
Russian tactical aviation threat noted on the north‑east direction, potentially affecting front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/66265).
UAV sightings in Sumy region include locations near Boromlya (east) and Shostka (south‑west) (Source: @eRadarrua/98073).
Ukrainian drones attacked fuel depots of Naftogaz in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv, and Dnipropetrovsk regions, halting operations (Source: @UkraineNow/82157).
Ukrainian UAVs observed near the main substation in Sevastopol, causing a fire and power outage (Source: @UkraineNow/82140).
Russian strikes reported in the Pavlovgrad district, part of the southern operational area (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
Ukrainian forces report using the "Vyravnyvatel" guided bomb, potentially targeting southern infrastructure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31125).
Ukrainian UAV activity noted in Dnipropetrovsk region near Aviatorske (Source: @eRadarrua/98053).
Russian tactical aviation activity was highlighted on the north‑east axis, suggesting possible air‑strike missions near front‑line zones (Source: @kpszsu/66265).
Ukrainian drone operations caused a major power outage in Sevastopol after multiple strikes on the main substation (Source: @UkraineNow/82140).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed conducting reconnaissance near Aviatorske in Dnipropetrovsk and over Sumy region (Source: @eRadarrua/98053; @kpszsu/66233).
Russian drones attacked civilian targets in Belgorod region, causing injuries in several villages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
Ukrainian forces reported using the "Vyravnyvatel" guided aerial bomb, indicating an expansion of air‑to‑ground capabilities (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31125).

key_developments: Ukrainian drone strikes disabled Sevastopol's power grid, igniting a fire at the main substation and prompting civilian warnings (Source: @UkraineNow/82140).
Russian overnight strikes hit Sumy, Kharkiv, and Pavlovgrad district, indicating a coordinated multi‑region offensive (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
Ukrainian small‑arms engagements continue in Shostka district and multiple Sumy villages, showing persistent ground combat (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31127).
Russian tactical aviation activity reported on the north‑east front, suggesting potential air‑strike escalation (Source: @kpszsu/66265).
Ukrainian UAV sightings proliferate across Sumy, Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, and Zaporizhzhia, reflecting intensified aerial reconnaissance (Source: @eRadarrua/98053; @kpszsu/66233).
Ukrainian drones attacked fuel depots of Naftogaz in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv, and Dnipropetrovsk, halting fuel operations (Source: @UkraineNow/82157).
Ukrainian forces introduced the "Vyravnyvatel" guided aerial bomb, expanding precision strike options (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31125).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian UAV capabilities are increasingly impacting strategic infrastructure, as seen in Sevastopol and fuel depots, while Russian forces maintain pressure through coordinated strikes across multiple fronts. The proliferation of UAV sightings suggests both sides are intensifying aerial reconnaissance and strike operations, raising the risk of rapid escalation. Continued small‑arms engagements in eastern villages indicate a grinding ground conflict that could be influenced by air‑to‑ground assets.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian leadership emphasizes the need for partner support to sustain operations, particularly in counter‑UAV measures and precision munitions. Russian statements highlight confidence in achieving operational goals, while tactical aviation threats suggest a focus on air superiority. Neighboring states monitor the increased UAV activity, with concerns over spill‑over effects on border regions.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitor UAV flight paths near critical infrastructure, especially power substations and fuel depots, for early warning of strikes. Track Russian tactical aviation deployments on the north‑east axis to anticipate potential air‑strike campaigns. Assess the operational impact of the new "Vyravnyvatel" bomb on Ukrainian strike capabilities and possible counter‑measures.
